meaning: adj.多卷的,卷帙浩瀚的(亦作multivolumed)
examples: The multivolume archive was created successfully.
The valve is also capable of placing the second chamber in fluid communication with the first chamber to provide a second volume range of the multivolume pipette.
Ni Ni is currently working on a multivolume modern history of Myanmar.
And, as Robert Caro's multivolume biography of Lyndon Johnson reminds us, Hispanics historically hold the keys to victory, at least in party primaries.
